WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
course_objectives: To familiarize students with the basic and advance Java Programming Language.
To learn modern tools to develop java-based web applications.
course_outcomes: CO1: Implement core Java concepts that model real world entities.
CO2: Develop programs based on new java features.
CO3: Apply a collection framework to build modular java programs.
CO4: Construct dynamic web pages using JDBC, Servlets and JSP.
CO5: Implement web and RESTful Web Services with Spring Boot using Spring Framework concepts.

units.title: Java Basics
topics: Introduction to Java, JVM, JRE, Java Environment
Java Source File Structure and Compilation
Defining Classes in Java, Constructors, Methods
Access Specifiers, Static Members, Final Members
Class, Object, Abstraction, Inheritance, Encapsulation, Polymorphism
Exceptions: try, catch, finally, throw, throws
In-built and User Defined Exceptions
Checked and Un-Checked Exceptions

units.title: Java Features
topics: Thread, Thread Life Cycle, Creating Threads, Thread Priorities
Functional Interfaces, Lambda Expression, Method References
Stream API, Default Methods, Static Method, For Each Method
Try-with-resources, Type Annotations, Repeating Annotations
Java Module System, Diamond Syntax with Inner Anonymous Class
Local Variable Type Inference, Switch Expressions, Yield Keyword
Records, Sealed Classes

units.title: Java Collections
topics: Collection in Java, Collection Framework Hierarchy
Iterator Interface, Collection Interface
List Interface: ArrayList, LinkedList, Vector, Stack
Queue Interface
Set Interface: HashSet, LinkedHashSet, TreeSet
Map Interface: HashMap, LinkedHashMap, TreeMap, Hashtable
Sorting, Comparable Interface, Comparator Interface
Properties Class in Java

units.title: Advance Java
topics: JDBC: Merging Data from Multiple Tables, Joins, Manipulating Databases
Prepared Statements, Transaction Processing, Stored Procedures
Servlet Overview and Architecture
Interface Servlet and the Servlet Life Cycle
Handling HTTP get and post Requests
Redirecting Requests, Session Tracking, Cookies
Session Tracking with HttpSession
Java Server Pages (JSP): Implicit Objects, Scripting, Standard Actions, Directives

units.title: Spring and Spring Boot
topics: MVC, Spring Core Basics, Spring Dependency Injection
Spring Inversion of Control, AOP
Bean Scopes: Singleton, Prototype, Request, Session, Application, Web Socket
Auto wiring, Annotations, Life Cycle Callbacks
Bean Configuration styles
Spring Boot Build Systems, Spring Boot Code Structure
Spring Boot Runners, Logger
Building RESTful Web Services: Rest Controller, Request Mapping
GET, POST, PUT, DELETE APIs
Build Web Applications

textbooks: Herbert Schildt - Java The complete reference, McGraw Hill Education
Steven Holzner - Java Black Book, Dreamtech
Balagurusamy E - Programming in Java, McGraw Hill
Java: A Beginner's Guide by Herbert Schildt, Oracle Press
Greg L. Turnquist - Learning Spring Boot 2.0 - Second Edition, Packt Publication
reference_books: Kathey Sierra - Head First Java, O'Reilly
AJ Henley Jr, Dave Wolf - Introduction to Java Spring Boot: Learning by Coding
Craig Walls - Spring Boot in Action, Manning Publication
